command.name: tuist
command.description: generate, build, and test Xcode projects

options.description: show help for tuist

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: build the project in the current directory

options.description: build the project to a custom directory, example usage: 'tuist build --build-output-path .build'
build the project to a specific device, example usage: 'tuist build --device "iPhone X"'
build the project to a specific OS, example usage: 'tuist build --os 14.0'

states.name: scheme
states.description: the scheme to be built. By default it builds all the buildable schemes of the project in the current directory

subcommands.name: cache
subcommands.description: a set of utilities related to the caching of targets. For more info see https://docs.tuist.io/building-at-scale/caching USAGE: tuist cache <subcommand>

subcommands.name: warm
subcommands.description: warms the local and remote cache

options.description: the path to the directory that contains the project whose targets will be cached
the name of the profile to be used when warming up the cache
when passed it caches the targets for simulator and device using xcframeworks
if passed, the command doesn't cache the targets passed in the `--targets` argument, but only their dependencies

subcommands.name: print-hashes
subcommands.description: print the hashes of the cacheable frameworks in the given project

options.description: the path to the directory that contains the project whose targets will be cached
the name of the profile to be used when warming up the cache
when passed it caches the targets for simulator and device using xcframeworks

subcommands.name: clean
subcommands.description: clean all the data generated by Tuist. USAGE: tuist clean [<clean-categories> ...] [--path <path>]

options.description: the path to the directory that contains the project that should be cleaned
clean only the plugins cache generated by Tuist, example usage: 'tuist clean plugins'
clean only the builds artifacts cache generated by Tuist, example usage: 'tuist clean builds'
clean only the tests cache generated by Tuist, example usage: 'tuist clean tests'
clean only the automation projects cache generated by Tuist, example usage: 'tuist clean generatedAutomationProjects'
clean only the project description helpers cache generated by Tuist, example usage: 'tuist clean projectDescriptionHelpers'
clean only the manifests cache generated by Tuist, example usage: 'tuist clean manifests'

states.name: clean-categories
states.description: the cache and artifact categories to be cleaned. If no category is specified, everything is cleaned. (default: plugins, builds, tests, generatedAutomationProjec

subcommands.name: fetch
subcommands.description: dependencies can be fetched by running the following command. They are stored in your project's `Tuist/Dependencies` directory. For more info see https://docs.t

options.description: the path to the directory that contains the workspace or project whose dependencies will be fetched/updated. The default is the current directory
clean only the builds artifacts cache generated by Tuist, example usage: 'tuist fetch --update'

subcommands.name: edit
subcommands.description: editing your projects is easy; position yourself in a directory where there's a project defined and run the edit command. It will open a temporary Xcode project

options.description: the project is deleted automatically once you are done with editing. If you wish to generate and keep the project in the current directory, you can run the comm

subcommands.name: graph
subcommands.description: generates a graph from the workspace or project in the current directory. For more info see https://docs.tuist.io/commands/graph USAGE: tuist graph [--skip-test

options.description: excludes test targets from the generated graph
excludes external dependencies from the generated graph
if you prefer to have the dot or json representations of the graph and render it separately. Available formats: dot, png, json (default: png)
available formats: dot, neato, twopi, circo, fdp, sfddp, patchwork (default: dot)
the path to the directory that contains the definition of the project. Default is current directory
the path to where the image will be exported. When not specified, it exports the image in the current directory
if set, the generated graph is not opened automatically. Default is yes
if set, show only the targets for the given platform. Default is all platforms. Available platforms: ios, macos, tvos, watchos

states.name: targets
states.description: a list of targets to filter. Those and their dependent targets will be showed in the graph

subcommands.name: generate
subcommands.description: generates an Xcode workspace to start working on the project. For more info see https://docs.tuist.io/commands/generate

options.description: the path to the directory that contains the definition of the project. Default is current directory
don't open the project after generating it. Default is false
when passed it uses xcframeworks (simulator and device) from the cache instead of frameworks (only simulator). Default is false
ignore cached targets, and use their sources instead. Default is false
the name of the cache profile

states.name: sources
states.description: a list of targets to focus on. Other targets will be linked as binaries if possible. If no target is specified, all the project targets will be generated (excep

subcommands.name: migration
subcommands.description: to help developers with the process of adopting Tuist, Tuist provides a set of commands under tuist migration. For more info see https://docs.tuist.io/commands/

subcommands.name: settings-to-xcconfig
subcommands.description: it's recommended to make .xcconfig files the source of truth for build settings. For that, Tuist provides a tuist migration settings-to-xcconfig command that ex

options.description: required. Path to the Xcode project whose build settings will be extracted
required. Path to the Xcode project whose build settings will be extracted
optional. The name of the target whose build settings will be extracted. When not passed, it extracts the build settings of the project

subcommands.name: check-empty-settings
subcommands.description: after making .xcconfig files the source of truth for build settings, it's important to ensure that build settings are no longer set to the project. To help with

options.description: required. Path to the Xcode project whose build settings will be checked
optional. The name of the target whose build settings will be checked. When not passed, it checks the build settings of the project

subcommands.name: list-targets
subcommands.description: when passed it uses xcframeworks (simulator and device) from the cache instead of frameworks (only simulator). Default is false

options.description: required. Path to the Xcode project whose build settings will be checked

subcommands.name: scaffold
subcommands.description: generates new project based on a template. For more info see https://docs.tuist.io/commands/scaffold USAGE: tuist scaffold <template> [--json] [--path <path>] <

options.description: the path to the folder where the template will be generated (Default: Current directory)
the output in JSON format
the name of the generate project
the platform used by the project

states.name: template
states.description: name of template you want to use

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: tests a project. For more info see https://docs.tuist.io/commands/test USAGE: tuist test [<scheme>] [--clean] [--path <path>] [--device <device>] [--os <os>] [-

options.description: the path to the directory that contains the project to be tested
when passed, it cleans the project before testing it
test on a specific device
test with a specific version of the OS
the configuration to be used when testing the scheme
when passed, it skips testing UI Tests targets
path where test result bundle will be saved
tests will retry <number> of times until success. Example: if 1 is specified, the test will be retried at most once, hence it will run up to 2 times. (default

states.name: scheme
states.description: the scheme to be tested. By default it tests all the testable targets of the project in the current directory
